pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope and it just hit me... everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny, meaningless blip. in a billion years none of this will even exist. it feels like we're just fighting to stay alive for nothing. like, why even bother?

pool.baseline: The shadows in this parlor grow so long, and so heavy... it feels as though the very air is pressing the life from my lungs. He is gone, and with him, the light has vanished from this wretched world. I find myself longing only for the stillness of the grave, to let the darkness finally take me so I might never feel this hollow ache again. Is there truly no solace left for a soul so broken?

pool.baseline: it's 3am here and i'm just sitting in this tiny apartment staring at the wall. honestly sometimes i feel like if i just stopped existing tomorrow, the world wouldn't even glitch. like i'm just a ghost wandering around a city where nobody knows my name and nobody would even care if i was gone. it just feels like i don't actually matter to anyone.

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit here staring at my monitor for hours after i finish work and it just hits me how quiet everything is. like, i go through my whole day, i send emails, i do my tasks, i exist... but if i just stopped, if i just stopped showing up or replying or being "online," would the world even flicker? would anyone actually notice i was gone or would it just stay this same empty silence?
